title: Maple Mustard Greek Yogurt Dressing
description: A tangy, protein-forward dressing built on 0% Greek yogurt instead of mayo or oil. Woodsy maple and sharp Dijon over chicken breast and spinach without spending your fat budget.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk the 1/2 cup (120 g) 0% plain Greek yogurt and 2 tbsp Dijon mustard until smooth.
Whisk in the 1 tbsp maple syrup and 1 tbsp lemon juice (about 1/2 lemon) until fully combined.
Stir in the 1 tsp extra virgin olive oil and 1/4 tsp garlic powder, then season with salt and pepper to taste.
Thin with water 1 tsp at a time if you want a more pourable consistency. Drizzle over chicken and spinach, or use as a dip.
notes: Macros are per serving (about 2 tbsp); the batch makes roughly 1/2 cup. Keeps 4-5 days refrigerated in a sealed jar. Maple is thinner than honey so go easy when adding water to adjust consistency. For a sharper version, drop the maple to 2 tsp and add an extra teaspoon of Dijon.
